paddle.kthvalue finds k-th smallest, tf.math.top_k finds k-th largest; they are inverse operations. To align semantics, TensorFlow must compute (n-k+1)-th smallest via top_k on negated input or use tf.math.top_k with k and then invert logic. But shape mismatch (PD=(2,2,1,1,4,1) vs TF=(2,2,1,1,4,5)) indicates axis handling differs: paddle.kthvalue with axis=-1 + keepdim=True outputs same rank with size-1 dim, while tf.math.top_k always reduces last dim to k and keeps rank — so TF output shape is [2,1,1,4,5], but paddle's is [2,1,1,4,1] due to k=5 on dim=10 with keepdim=True. To fix: remove keepdim in paddle or add equivalent dimension expansion in TF; but per doc, tf.math.top_k has no keepdims — so paddle must set keepdim=False to match TF's default squeezing behavior.
